steps.title: Pin down the claim
steps.content: The banker is described as "very upset about something." The supporting quote must show visible emotional distress — tears, agitation, sleeplessness, etc.

steps.title: Find the quote that shows distress
steps.content: The matching choice shows the banker lying on his bed while "his tears and emotion kept him for hours from sleeping." Tears plus hours of sleeplessness directly illustrate being very upset.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Then the banker cautiously broke the seals off the door and put the key in the keyhole.": describes a careful action, not an emotional state; no distress shown. ✗
  • Choice B — "It struck three o'clock, the banker listened; everyone was asleep in the house and nothing could be heard outside but the rustling of the chilled trees.": sets a quiet nighttime scene; says nothing about the banker's feelings. ✗
  • Choice C — "The banker, spoilt and frivolous, with millions beyond his reckoning, was delighted at the bet.": shows the opposite emotion — delight, not upset. ✗
  • Choice D — "When [the banker] got home he lay on his bed, but his tears and emotion kept him for hours from sleeping.": tears and sleeplessness clearly portray a banker who is very upset. ✓
D is correct.
